arts.ttl: Arsenal send scout to Thursday Europa League clash – Transfer planning may be underway
arts.sum: Arsenal were one of the sides to send a scout to watch Porto take on Roma in the Europa League on Thursday evening. That’s according to O Jogo and Record, who both report that Arsenal had a scout in attendance for the European clash. arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy told what Leicester City must do against 'rocked' Arsenal
arts.sum: Leicester City should have a go at Arsenal and hope to catch them feeling sorry for themselves amid their attacking injuries, former Gunners midfielder Paul Merson has said. “This is a good game for Arsenal even though they’ve been rocked by the injury to Kai Havertz,” Merson said in his Sportskeeda column.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta blames packed schedule for Kai Havertz injury: ‘There’s no time for training’
arts.sum: The German, who is set to miss the rest of the season, is the third Arsenal forward sidelined by a hamstring injury

arts.ttl: Arteta admits Arsenal have made free agent U-TURN after Havertz injury
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has admitted that his side could dive into the free agent market to sign a centre-forward in order to cover for injuries to Kai Havertz and Gabriel Jesus. The Gunners have seen both their senior options for the No.9 role struck down with serious injury in recent weeks, leaving them short of an obvious pick.

arts.ttl: Arsenal predicted XI vs Leicester as Mikel Arteta uses rogue option to solve injury crisis
arts.sum: Arsenal will be without Kai Havertz, Gabriel Jesus, Gabriel Martinelli and Bukayo Saka as they attempt to keep their title charge alive with three points at Leicester

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta insists Arsenal can cope with Kai Havertz injury and provides update on Bukayo Saka return
arts.sum: The German forward was ruled out for the remainder of the season after suffering a hamstring injury at a warm weather training camp in Dubai

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta sheds more light on Kai Havertz injury and discusses Arsenal replacements
arts.sum: Arsenal have been dealt another injury blow with Kai Havertz ruled out for the season as Mikel Arteta's attacking options take another hit after a quiet January transfer window

arts.ttl: Arsenal injury latest, Slot’s derby regret, Premier League news and more: football – live
arts.sum: Latest news ahead of another football weekend Upcoming fixtures | Read Football Daily | And mail John Sad news reached us this morning of the passing of Patrick Barclay – Paddy to many – a former Guardian and Observer journalist. Those of us who knew him – and everyone knew Paddy – will miss the tales, the laughter, the usually unshakeable, strident opinions and the love for football, particularly Dundee FC. Paddy, a skilful writer, an opinion-former, worked for the whole broadsheet spread of Guardian, Times, Telegraph and Independent over his long career and was later the president of the Football Writers’ Association. A true Fleet Street veteran of the old school who also made time for the younger crew who had grown up reading his reports. It was always a pleasure to talk football – and much else besides – with him. He will be sorely missed. Arteta on that weight of injuries: “With the loads and minutes, it’s inevitable, It’s an accident waiting to happen. This season it’s the accumulation of that, the stress. Is it luck? Certainly the schedule is demanding, and for explosive players it becomes an issue.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal given crucial advantage over Man United in race to sign 38 G/A man
arts.sum: Manchester United reportedly look like an unlikely destination for Sporting Lisbon striker Viktor Gyokeres as things stand. The Sweden international has been on fire for Sporting this season, contributing a stunning 34 goals and four assists for the Portuguese giants in all competitions. arts.ttl: Arsenal handed worrying Saka injury timeline as Isak transfer stance set
arts.sum: The news comes just as Arsenal announce that Kai Havertz will miss the rest of the season after suffering a similar injury. It would be an easy thing to do after Havertz’s injury to try and fast-track Saka’s recovery, but the last thing Arsenal need if for him to come back and instantly be out again for the rest of the season.
